title: The Alarm Clock.
content:
With a clatter and a jangle,
        And a wrangle and a screech,
     How the old alarm clock wheezes
        As it sneezes out of reach!
     How you groan and yawn and stretch
        In the chilly morning air,
     As you pull the blankets tight,
        With your head clear out of sight--
     How you swear!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edwin C. Ranck, titled "The Alarm Clock.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
